summary: Jeff Atwood reviews Robert Glass's 'Facts and Fallacies of Software Engineering,' finding value in its lesser-known insights despite criticizing its writing style and repetition of well-known concepts. He highlights the 'Rule of Three' for code reuse: building reusable components is three times harder than single-use ones, and code should be tested across three different applications before being considered truly reusable. Atwood argues that this rule actually underestimates the difficulty, claiming truly reusable code is an order of magnitude harder to write. He warns developers against prematurely building 'general purpose' solutions for hypothetical future needs, calling this the 'delusion of reuse.'
key_points: Building reusable components is at least three times harder than single-use components, and Atwood believes it's actually an order of magnitude harder
Code should be tested in three different applications with different audiences before being considered truly reusable
Developers often fall into the trap of building 'general purpose' solutions for unknown future projects that may never materialize
Sometimes resisting the urge to write reusable code is the right choice for getting current projects done
You cannot validate reusability without actually attempting reuse across different developers and problem domains
money_quotes: I believe writing a truly reusable class is an order of magnitude harder than writing a single use class.
Sometimes the right thing to do is resist the urge to write 'general purpose' solutions.
How confident are you that the so-called 'general purpose' solution you built will actually work for these… unknown future projects? Have you tried it?
You can't know if you have a strong case for reuse unless you've tried – and possibly failed – to use that same bit of code on at least three different projects, with three different audiences.
Until you've invested the additional effort to implement that 'reusable' code with different developers and different problem domains, all you have is the delusion of reuse.
I've seen too many developers fall into this trap. Myself included.
key_insight: True code reusability can only be validated through actual reuse across multiple projects—anything else is premature abstraction and self-deception.
